_meta.notes: Update this file and push to main to distribute new product data to all VASTbuilder users without redeployment. Fetched by Update KB button via raw.githubusercontent.com.

cboxModels.name: VAST CBox (Standard) Gen 6
cboxModels.notes: Stateless compute. No local storage. Includes VAST AI OS. Recommended for all new deployments.

cboxModels.name: VAST CBox (Standard) Gen 5
cboxModels.notes: Previous generation. Supported under VAST 3-generation policy.

cboxModels.name: VAST CNode-X (GPU-Accelerated)
cboxModels.description: AI inference node with Blackwell GPUs — 2U
cboxModels.gpus: 2x NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (96GB GDDR7 each)
cboxModels.partnerModel: Supermicro SYS-212GB-FNR, Cisco UCS C845A M8
cboxModels.notes: Runs CUDA-accelerated VAST AI OS services: vector search, SQL analytics, RAG pipelines, NIM microservices. Announced Feb 2026 at VAST Forward.

dboxModels.name: VAST Ceres DF-3015V2
dboxModels.notes: PSU 2x1600W redundant. Active draw 400-728W typical. Standard-density D-Node.

dboxModels.name: VAST Ceres DF-3060V2
dboxModels.notes: Highest density per rack unit. PSU 2x1600W redundant.

dboxModels.name: VAST Voyager
dboxModels.notes: Confirm availability with VAST. Primary DBox line is Ceres DF-30xx as of 2026 datasheets. Voyager may refer to a prior product generation or specific config.

dboxModels.name: VAST EBox (Supermicro) Gen 2
dboxModels.notes: EBox Gen 2 (2025-2026). Runs VAST AI OS on x86. Requires VastOS 5.2+. HPE GreenLake managed service available.

dboxModels.name: VAST EBox (Cisco AI POD)
dboxModels.partnerModel: Cisco UCS C225 M8, Cisco UCS C845A M8
dboxModels.notes: Part of Cisco Secure AI Factory reference architecture. Cisco Nexus Hyperfabric AI integration.

switchModels.name: Arista 7050CX3-32S
switchModels.notes: Standard VAST backend leaf switch. Enable PFC CoS3, ECN, MTU9000.

switchModels.name: Arista 7060CX2-32S
switchModels.notes: Validated VAST backend leaf. Enable arp gratuitous accept for VIP failover.

switchModels.name: Arista 7800R3
switchModels.notes: Spine switch for large deployments. Deep buffer for lossless RDMA.

switchModels.name: Arista 7800R4
switchModels.notes: 2025-2026 generation spine. AI factory scale deployments. Arista Etherlink portfolio.

switchModels.name: NVIDIA Spectrum-4 SN5600
switchModels.notes: Spectrum-X platform. Recommended for VAST CNode backend in NVIDIA AI reference architectures.

switchModels.name: NVIDIA QM8700 IB HDR
switchModels.notes: HDR InfiniBand backend. For IB-fabric VAST clusters.

switchModels.name: Cisco Nexus 9336C-FX2
switchModels.notes: Part of Cisco Secure AI Factory. Nexus Hyperfabric AI integration.

switchModels.name: Cisco Nexus 93600CD-GX
switchModels.notes: High-density Cisco AI POD design leaf.

switchModels.name: Generic RoCEv2 Eth Switch
switchModels.notes: Minimum requirement: PFC, ECN, MTU9000. Must pass VAST network validation test.

VMware vSphere.notes: VAAI-NAS: Full File Clone, Native Snapshot, Extended Stats, Reserve Space. VAAI Block (NVMe-TCP): XCOPY, VMFS ATS. Max 2 PiB per datastore for Storage DRS. Enable arp gratuitous accept on network.

Kubernetes CSI.notes: RWX + RWO volumes. Dynamic provisioning. Prometheus metrics. OperatorHub deploy. Helm: helm repo add vastcsi https://vast-data.github.io/vast-csi

OpenStack Manila.notes: NFS share driver. Standard CRUD, access rules.

NVIDIA GPUDirect Storage.notes: DMA from VAST NVMe storage directly to GPU VRAM. NFS over RDMA primary path. New in 5.5: S3 over RDMA for AI pipelines.

CoreWeave.notes: $1.17B multi-year commercial agreement (Nov 6, 2025). VAST AI OS is primary data foundation for CoreWeave AI cloud.

Google Cloud.notes: First fully managed VAST AI OS on GCP (Nov 11, 2025). DataSpace unified global namespace. TPU VM integration.

Microsoft Azure.notes: Agentic AI collaboration. Azure-native VAST experience. InsightEngine + AgentEngine on Azure.

AWS.notes: VAST AI OS on AWS. Polaris on AWS in 5.4.5 (June 2026). Hybrid cloud scaling.

ai-ml-training.name: AI/ML Training
ai-ml-training.notes: GPUDirect Storage + NFS over RDMA. Use nconnect=16 on Linux mounts. elbencho for benchmarking.

ai-training.name: AI/ML Training
ai-training.notes: GPUDirect Storage + NFS over RDMA. Use nconnect=16 on Linux mounts.

agentic-ai-rag.name: Agentic AI / RAG
agentic-ai-rag.notes: CNode-X recommended. Trillion-vector index (VastOS 5.5+). S3 over RDMA. PolicyEngine for agentic governance. RAG pipelines via VAST Foundation Stacks.

hpc-genomics.name: HPC & Genomics
hpc-genomics.notes: High parallel read from many HPC nodes. InfiniBand option for MPI-heavy workloads.

vmware-vsphere.name: VMware/vSphere
vmware-vsphere.notes: VAAI-NAS offload. Multiple VIPs for NFS multipathing. Max 2 PiB per datastore. Enable arp gratuitous accept.

kubernetes-csi.name: Kubernetes CSI
kubernetes-csi.notes: VAST CSI v2.6.5. RWX + RWO. OperatorHub deploy. Helm chart available. K8s 1.22-1.31.6.

backup-archive.name: Backup & Archive
backup-archive.notes: High compression on backup streams. WORM for compliance. S3 API for backup target.

openstack.name: OpenStack
openstack.notes: Cinder NVMe-TCP block + Manila NFS file. Requires VastOS 5.3+. Yoga+ validated.

media-vfx.name: Media & VFX
media-vfx.notes: Large sequential I/O. Low DRR (already-compressed media). NFSv3/SMB mix typical.

hybrid-dr.name: Hybrid / DR
hybrid-dr.notes: Async/sync VAST Mirror replication. VAST on GCP/AWS/Azure as DR target. WAN bandwidth planning critical.

database-analytics.name: Database / Analytics
database-analytics.notes: Native SQL via Trino on CNode (VastOS 5.4+). High write rate with strong compression on structured data.

file-share.name: Enterprise File Shares
file-share.notes: Mixed NFS/SMB workload. SMB encryption (5.4+). Kerberos auth (5.4+). Good all-round DRR.
